current_wind.description: Tailwind/Crosswind from SE, gusts to 12.1 mph.

bh.shot_strategy: Player, this is a headwind. A clean RHBH with an overstable disc should hold true. Focus on a controlled hyzer angle.

fh.shot_strategy: A RHFH with an overstable disc will also cut through this headwind effectively. Aim for a smooth release.

bh.shot_strategy: Champ, aim for a controlled LHBH with an overstable disc. The headwind will help keep it straight.

fh.shot_strategy: For LHFH, an overstable disc will work well here. Focus on a clean release to combat the headwind.

bh.shot_strategy: Player, this slight crosswind from the SE will push your disc right. A clean RHBH with a stable disc on a hyzer should handle it well.

fh.shot_strategy: A RHFH with a stable disc is also a solid option here, working with the wind.

bh.shot_strategy: Champ, a LHBH with an understable disc on an anhyzer should fight this crosswind and shape nicely towards the target.

fh.shot_strategy: For LHFH, a stable disc on a controlled line is your best bet here.

bh.shot_strategy: Player, this is a strong crosswind from your right. RHBH is risky as it exposes the underside. Prefer a RHFH with an overstable disc, keeping it low.

fh.shot_strategy: A RHFH with an overstable disc is strongly recommended here. Focus on a clean, controlled release to combat the wind.

bh.shot_strategy: Champ, the wind from the right and the natural fade of an LHBH will push you OB. A strong LHFH with an overstable disc is the play.

fh.shot_strategy: For LHFH, lean on an overstable disc. Aim to keep it flat or with a slight hyzer to counteract the wind pushing right.

fh.shot_strategy: Use an overstable driver for your RHFH. Focus on a clean release to maximize control against the wind.

bh.shot_strategy: Champ, the wind is pushing hard from the right. An overstable disc for your LHFH will be most effective here. Avoid any anhyzer.
